vocabulary.explanation: Relating to a galaxy or outer space on a vast scale. In the film, 'galactic' describes the cosmic scope of Mario's adventure — worlds, enemies, and challenges that exist far beyond Earth or the Mushroom Kingdom, spanning entire star systems.

A small, planet-like celestial body. Derived directly from the Super Mario Galaxy games, planetoids are the signature bite-sized worlds Mario runs across and around — a visual concept central to the film's unique aesthetic and level design-inspired environments.

A glowing, collectible star object from the Mario Galaxy games that serves as the primary goal of each world. In the movie context, Power Stars likely function as MacGuffins — powerful items Mario must seek to save the cosmos, driving the film's plot forward.

A star-shaped catapult mechanism from the Mario Galaxy games that shoots characters across space at high speed. Expect this to be a major visual set piece in the film, used for dynamic interstellar travel sequences between cosmic worlds.

The phenomenon of counteracting gravitational pull, allowing objects or characters to move freely in space or along curved surfaces. Anti-gravity is a defining mechanic of the Galaxy games and will likely be used in the film for inventive action choreography and visually stunning sequences.

The fictional homeland of Mario, Princess Peach, and the Toads — the colorful, cheerful realm familiar from decades of Mario games. In this film, it serves as the 'home base' Mario leaves behind, grounding the audience before the cosmic adventure begins.

An English idiom meaning extraordinarily impressive or amazing. It carries a double meaning in this film — both the literal fact that Mario's adventure takes place in outer space and a colloquial expression of how spectacular and mind-blowing the cosmic environments and action sequences are.

A key character from the Super Mario Galaxy games — a tall, ethereal cosmic guardian who watches over the universe from her Observatory. She is widely expected to appear in the film and plays a pivotal narrative and emotional role in the Galaxy saga, serving as a mentor figure to Mario.

background.summary: In The Super Mario Galaxy Movie (2026), Mario leaves the beloved Mushroom Kingdom behind and launches into the cosmos, embarking on a breathtaking interstellar adventure. Exploring vibrant, gravity-defying cosmic worlds filled with alien landscapes, celestial puzzles, and galactic enemies, Mario must overcome challenges unlike anything he has faced before. The film blends high-energy action, heartfelt moments, and signature Nintendo charm, promising a universe-spanning quest that will delight longtime fans and newcomers alike.
context: The film is based on Nintendo's acclaimed 'Super Mario Galaxy' video game series (2007, 2010), celebrated for its imaginative spherical planetoid level design and orchestral soundtrack.
This is a follow-up to the box-office hit 'The Super Mario Bros. Movie' (2023), which grossed over $1.3 billion worldwide and revived mainstream interest in video game adaptations.
The 'Super Mario' franchise, created by Shigeru Miyamoto, is one of the most iconic and best-selling video game series in history, with Mario as Nintendo's global mascot since 1981.
'Galaxy' themes in the movie draw from concepts like anti-gravity, launch stars, and power stars — core mechanics of the source game — reimagined as visual and narrative storytelling devices.
Chris Pratt returns as Mario, Anya Taylor-Joy as Princess Peach, and Benny Safdie joins the cast, continuing the franchise's blend of Hollywood star power with beloved game characters.
background.director_note: Aaron Horvath and Michael Jelenic are longtime creative collaborators best known for their work on the Emmy-winning animated series 'Teen Titans Go!' (Cartoon Network, 2013–present), where they served as writers and producers. Their storytelling style is characterized by irreverent humor, rapid-fire comedy, self-aware meta jokes, and vibrant pop-culture references layered over earnest, character-driven heart. Together, they co-directed 'Teen Titans GO! To the Movies' (2018), a critically praised animated feature that demonstrated their ability to scale their comedic sensibility to the big screen while delivering genuine emotional resonance. Their work on 'The Super Mario Bros. Movie' (2023) marked their foray into major franchise filmmaking, where they balanced fan service, slapstick action, and broad family appeal at a blockbuster level. Horvath and Jelenic tend to favor fast-paced editing, expressive character animation, and a tone that winks at the audience without undermining the story's stakes — a style perfectly suited to the whimsical, adventurous spirit of the Mario universe.
background.wikipedia: The Super Mario Galaxy Movie is a 2026 animated adventure comedy film based on the 2007 video game Super Mario Galaxy and its 2010 sequel, as well as Nintendo's broader Mario franchise. The sequel to The Super Mario Bros. Movie (2023), it was directed by Aaron Horvath and Michael Jelenic and written by Matthew Fogel. Chris Pratt, Anya Taylor-Joy, Charlie Day, Jack Black and Keegan-Michael Key reprise their roles, with Benny Safdie, Donald Glover, and Brie Larson joining the cast. It is produced by Illumination and Nintendo. In the film, Mario and Luigi and their friends adventure into outer space, where they meet Princess Rosalina and face off against Bowser and his son, Bowser Jr.
